The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260513T184225-b293b8a9/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260513T184225-b293b8a9/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260513T184225-b293b8a9/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260513T184225-b293b8a9/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260513T184225-b293b8a9/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-07f1b92c">     <title>workerprobe: serialize JSONL tee writes under concurrent loop events</title>     <description> PROBLEM
The pre-push/full-test gate can fail under `-race` with a data race in workerprobe JSONL mirroring. This blocks otherwise unrelated worker landings and pushes, and it undermines the reliability of the worker-status/liveness work that depends on the same event path.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/workerprobe/probe.go:465-484` defines `teeWriter` with a mutable `buf []byte`, but `Write` appends to and slices that buffer without synchronization.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2718-2736` calls `sink.Write(line)` and then `sink.Write(newline)`. Multiple execute-loop goroutines can write through the same sink during cleanup/progress emission.
- Observed race evidence from the 2026-05-13 pre-push/full-test gate: `TestWorkDefaultDrainModeExitsOnEmptyQueue` in `cli/cmd/work_test.go:371-387` failed under `-race`; one goroutine wrote `workerprobe.(*teeWriter).Write` from `writeLoopEvent` during `ExecuteBeadWorker.Run`, while another wrote through the same tee from `runExecutionCleanupPass`.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the workerprobe JSONL tee concurrency-safe and preserve event parsing semantics. Add a mutex or equivalent serialization around `teeWriter.Write` buffer mutation and dispatch, and consider making `writeLoopEvent` emit the JSON line plus newline in a single `Write` to reduce interleaving. The fix must be local to event mirroring; structured logging must remain fail-open and must not block work if the probe/server is unavailable.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change worker scheduling, readiness classification, or server ingest contracts. Do not disable the race detector or loosen pre-push gates.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. `TestWorkerProbeTeeJSONLConcurrentWrites` or equivalent repeatedly writes newline-delimited loop events from multiple goroutines through one `TeeJSONL` writer under `-race` and proves events are parsed without data races.
2. `TestWriteLoopEventWritesAtomicallyToWorkerProbe` or equivalent proves a loop event plus newline is not interleaved into malformed JSON when concurrent cleanup/progress emitters share the same sink.
3. `TestWorkDefaultDrainModeExitsOnEmptyQueue` remains green under `-race`.
4. `cd cli && go test -race ./internal/agent/workerprobe/... ./cmd/... -run "TestWorkerProbeTeeJSONLConcurrentWrites|TestWriteLoopEventWritesAtomicallyToWorkerProbe|TestWorkDefaultDrainModeExitsOnEmptyQueue" -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>area:agent, area:workerprobe, area:server, kind:bug, phase:build, reliability, race</labels>     <metadata spec-id="FEAT-006" base-rev="4dd9102802f2ff6cb93713b3c886e67d5be56291" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260513T184225-b293b8a9"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <ref id="FEAT-006" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md">Feature: DDx Consumer of the Fizeau Execution Contract</ref>   </governing> </execute-bead>

data.text:
Implemented and verified.

- Serialized `teeWriter.Write` with a mutex and kept line parsing under that lock in [/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-07f1b92c-20260513T184225-b293b8a9/cli/internal/agent/workerprobe/probe.go].
- Made `writeLoopEvent` emit a single JSONL frame per call in [/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-07f1b92c-20260513T184225-b293b8a9/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go].
- Added the regression test for concurrent TeeJSONL writes in [/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-07f1b92c-20260513T184225-b293b8a9/cli/internal/agent/workerprobe/probe_race_test.go].
- Added the atomic framing regression test in [/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-07f1b92c-20260513T184225-b293b8a9/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_write_test.go].

Validation:
- `go test -race ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TestWorkerProbeTeeJSONLConcurrentWrites|TestWriteLoopEventWritesAtomicallyToWorkerProbe|TestWorkDefaultDrainModeExitsOnEmptyQueue' -count=1`
- `lefthook run pre-commit`

Commit:
- `d735c89e5 fix(workerprobe): serialize JSONL tee writes under concurrent loop events [ddx-07f1b92c]`
